evals.prompt: I want to add Smart Label Capture to my .NET for iOS app. Here's my empty view controller: EmptyViewController.cs. My labels are retail price tags — each has a product barcode (EAN-13 and Code 128) and an expiry date, plus an optional total price. I want to read all three fields in one scan and show them.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit NuGet versions, and writes complete Label Capture integration into the view controller: DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey; a manually-managed Camera via Camera.GetDefaultCamera(LabelCapture.RecommendedCameraSettings) + dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync; a label built field-by-field with CustomBarcode.Builder().SetSymbologies(new List<Symbology>{...}).Build(name), ExpiryDateText.Builder()...Build(name), TotalPriceText.Builder().IsOptional(true).Build(name); LabelDefinition.Create(name, fields); LabelCaptureSettings.Create(new List<LabelDefinition>{def}); LabelCapture.Create(context, settings); DataCaptureView.Create(context, this.View.Bounds) added via this.View.AddSubview + LabelCaptureBasicOverlay.Create(labelCapture) + dataCaptureView.AddOverlay(overlay); a SessionUpdated event (or ILabelCaptureListener) reading session.CapturedLabels, matching fields by name, reading Barcode?.Data / Text, setting labelCapture.Enabled=false, dispatching via UIApplication.SharedApplication.InvokeOnMainThread / DispatchQueue.MainQueue; camera On/Off + Enabled across ViewWillAppear/ViewWillDisappear. Shows the setup checklist (three NuGet packages incl. Scandit.DataCapture.Label, NSCameraUsageDescription, AppDelegate with the three Initialize() calls, SupportedOSPlatformVersion 15.0, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding Scandit.DataCapture.Core, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode, and Scandit.DataCapture.Label NuGet packages
It does NOT add a separate label-text-models / *.text-models package (text recognizers are bundled in Scandit.DataCapture.Label)
It does NOT add any *.Maui package (this is a non-MAUI .NET iOS project)
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Info.plist (and does NOT introduce an Android-style runtime camera-permission helper)
Setup checklist mentions calling ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize(), ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize(), AND ScanditLabelCapture.Initialize() (all three) in AppDelegate.FinishedLaunching (SDK 8.0+)
Setup checklist mentions setting SupportedOSPlatformVersion to at least 15.0
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
Each field is built with its own static Builder() factory ending in .Build("name") (e.g. CustomBarcode.Builder()...Build(...), ExpiryDateText.Builder()...Build(...))
The native fluent LabelCaptureSettings.builder()/addLabel()/buildFluent(...) chain is NOT used
CustomBarcode.Builder().SetSymbologies(...) is given an IList<Symbology> (e.g. new List<Symbology>{...}), not a vararg
Symbology.Ean13Upca and Symbology.Code128 are enabled for the barcode field
Swift-style symbology names (Symbology.ean13UPCA, .code128) are NOT used
An ExpiryDateText field is created via ExpiryDateText.Builder()
The total price field is created via TotalPriceText.Builder() and marked optional with .IsOptional(true)
LabelDefinition.Create(name, fields) is used to assemble the fields into a definition
LabelCaptureSettings.Create( is called with a list/IList<LabelDefinition> (NOT a settings builder)
LabelCapture.Create(context, settings) is used as the factory (NOT new LabelCapture(...) and NOT LabelCapture.forDataCaptureContext)
A Camera is obtained via Camera.GetDefaultCamera(...) using LabelCapture.RecommendedCameraSettings
dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync( is called with the camera (the camera is managed explicitly)
camera.SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.On) and FrameSourceState.Off (or Standby) drive the camera across ViewWillAppear/ViewWillDisappear
labelCapture.Enabled is set to true so frames are processed
DataCaptureView.Create( is called with the context AND a CGRect frame (e.g. this.View.Bounds), NOT the single-argument Android form
The DataCaptureView is added to the hierarchy with this.View.AddSubview(...) (NOT Android's container.AddView)
LabelCaptureBasicOverlay.Create(labelCapture) is created and added via dataCaptureView.AddOverlay(...)
Either labelCapture.SessionUpdated += handler or labelCapture.AddListener(ILabelCaptureListener) is wired
The handler reads session.CapturedLabels and returns early when it is empty
Fields are matched by their declared Name and values read via Barcode?.Data and Text
labelCapture.Enabled is set to false after a label is captured to avoid re-capturing
UI work is dispatched to the main thread via UIApplication.SharedApplication.InvokeOnMainThread or DispatchQueue.MainQueue (NOT Android's RunOnUiThread)
ViewDidLoad is NOT made async void (camera setup is fire-and-forget) OR the controller boilerplate guidance is respected

evals.prompt: I already have Smart Label Capture working in my .NET iOS app (see IntegratedViewController.cs). I need to add one more field to the same label: a lot number printed as plain text that always matches the pattern LOT-[0-9]{6} (e.g. LOT-004821). It's required. Then read it alongside the others.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a CustomText field built with CustomText.Builder().SetValueRegex("LOT-[0-9]{6}").Build("Lot Number"), inserts it into the existing fields list, and reads it via field Text in OnSessionUpdated, preserving the rest of the integration. It uses SetValueRegex (NOT the old native setPattern/setDataTypePattern).

assertions.text: A CustomText field is created via CustomText.Builder()...Build("<name>")
The value pattern is set with SetValueRegex("LOT-[0-9]{6}") (or SetValueRegexes with that pattern)
The old native names setPattern / setDataTypePattern / setPatterns are NOT used
The new field is added to the existing List<LabelFieldDefinition> (it is NOT marked optional, since it is required)
The lot number value is read from the captured label via field.Text matching its declared name
Existing integration code (camera, LabelCapture.Create, DataCaptureView.Create with CGRect, overlay, SessionUpdated handler, existing barcode and expiry fields) is preserved
No Android APIs (RunOnUiThread, container.AddView, single-arg DataCaptureView.Create) are introduced

evals.prompt: I have Smart Label Capture working in my .NET iOS app (see IntegratedViewController.cs) with the Basic Overlay. I want to highlight the barcode field in teal and the expiry date field in red, hide the whole-label outline, and open a detail screen when the user taps a label. Show me how.
evals.expected_output: The skill customizes the LabelCaptureBasicOverlay appearance per field/label by implementing ILabelCaptureBasicOverlayListener: BrushForField returns a teal Brush for the barcode field name and a red Brush for the expiry date field name (matching by field.Name), BrushForLabel returns Brush.TransparentBrush to hide the label outline, and OnLabelTapped handles the tap. The listener is assigned via overlay.Listener. Brushes are constructed with new Brush(fillColor, strokeColor, strokeWidth) using UIColor. The existing label definition, camera, mode, and SessionUpdated handler are preserved. No Android APIs are introduced.

assertions.text: A class implementing ILabelCaptureBasicOverlayListener is created
BrushForField(LabelCaptureBasicOverlay, LabelField, CapturedLabel) is implemented and returns different brushes based on field.Name (e.g. the barcode field name vs the expiry date field name)
BrushForLabel(LabelCaptureBasicOverlay, CapturedLabel) is implemented and returns Brush.TransparentBrush to hide the label outline
OnLabelTapped(LabelCaptureBasicOverlay, CapturedLabel) is implemented to handle the tap
Brushes are constructed with new Brush(...) using UIColor for the fill/stroke colors (not an Android-style brush)
The listener is assigned to the overlay via overlay.Listener = ...
The existing label definition, camera setup, LabelCapture mode, and SessionUpdated/listener handler are preserved
No Android APIs (RunOnUiThread, Java.Lang.Object listener base, container.AddView, single-arg DataCaptureView.Create) are introduced

evals.prompt: I have Smart Label Capture working in my .NET iOS app (see IntegratedViewController.cs). When a captured expiry date is within 3 days of today, I want to draw a custom 'Expires soon!' warning view anchored just below the expiry date field in the camera feed. How do I add that kind of AR-style overlay?
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a LabelCaptureAdvancedOverlay via LabelCaptureAdvancedOverlay.Create(labelCapture), attaches it with dataCaptureView.AddOverlay(advancedOverlay), and sets advancedOverlay.Listener to a class implementing ILabelCaptureAdvancedOverlayListener. ViewForCapturedLabelField returns a custom UIView (e.g. a UILabel) for the expiry date field (matching by Name and LabelFieldType.Text) and null otherwise, ViewForCapturedLabel returns null, with AnchorForCapturedLabelField returning Anchor.BottomCenter and OffsetForCapturedLabelField returning a PointWithUnit. It uses Create (not the native NewInstance) and preserves the existing setup.

assertions.text: LabelCaptureAdvancedOverlay.Create(labelCapture) is used to create the advanced overlay
The advanced overlay is attached via dataCaptureView.AddOverlay(...)
A class implementing ILabelCaptureAdvancedOverlayListener is created and set as advancedOverlay.Listener
ViewForCapturedLabelField(LabelCaptureAdvancedOverlay, LabelField) returns a custom UIView (e.g. a UILabel) for the expiry date field and null for other fields
The expiry date field is matched by field.Name and (optionally) field.Type == LabelFieldType.Text
AnchorForCapturedLabelField returns an Anchor value and OffsetForCapturedLabelField returns a PointWithUnit
The native init factory LabelCaptureAdvancedOverlay.NewInstance(...) is NOT used (Create is used instead)
The existing label definition, camera, mode, and result handler are preserved

evals.prompt: I'm building a .NET iOS app to scan hard-disk-drive labels and smart-device labels. The HDD labels have a serial number and a part number (both barcodes), and the device labels carry IMEI1 and IMEI2. I'd rather not hand-write the symbologies and regexes. Here's my empty view controller: EmptyViewController.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill uses the pre-built barcode field types instead of CustomBarcode with manual regexes: SerialNumberBarcode.Builder().Build(name), PartNumberBarcode.Builder().Build(name), ImeiOneBarcode.Builder().Build(name), ImeiTwoBarcode.Builder().Build(name). They are collected into a List<LabelFieldDefinition>, assembled via LabelDefinition.Create(name, fields) and LabelCaptureSettings.Create(...). Values are read via field.Barcode?.Data matching by Name. It does NOT use the native init factories (FieldWithName / InitWithName) and provides the full integration (context, camera, view, overlay, handler, checklist).

assertions.text: A serial number field is built via SerialNumberBarcode.Builder()...Build("<name>")
A part number field is built via PartNumberBarcode.Builder()...Build("<name>")
An IMEI1 field is built via ImeiOneBarcode.Builder()...Build("<name>")
An IMEI2 field is built via ImeiTwoBarcode.Builder()...Build("<name>")
The native init factories (SerialNumberBarcode.FieldWithName / ImeiOneBarcode.InitWithName / FieldWithName) are NOT used
The fields are assembled via LabelDefinition.Create(name, fields) and LabelCaptureSettings.Create(new List<LabelDefinition>{...})
Each pre-built barcode field value is read via field.Barcode?.Data matching by its declared Name
A license key placeholder string is present and LabelCapture.Create(context, settings) is used

evals.prompt: I want to scan retail price tags in my .NET iOS app and I don't want to define the individual fields myself. Is there a ready-made label definition for price labels? Show me how to use it. Here's my view controller: EmptyViewController.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill uses the pre-built price-capture label definition LabelDefinition.CreatePriceCaptureDefinition("<name>") rather than building Custom/TotalPrice fields by hand, passes it to LabelCaptureSettings.Create(new List<LabelDefinition>{ priceLabel }), and wires the rest of the integration (LabelCapture.Create, camera, DataCaptureView with CGRect + LabelCaptureBasicOverlay, SessionUpdated handler reading Text/Barcode?.Data). It may mention the other prebuilt definitions (CreateVinLabelDefinition, CreateSevenSegmentDisplayLabelDefinition).

assertions.text: LabelDefinition.CreatePriceCaptureDefinition("<name>") is used to create the price-label definition
The prebuilt definition is passed to LabelCaptureSettings.Create(new List<LabelDefinition>{...})
LabelCapture.Create(context, settings) is used as the factory
The DataCaptureView is created with a CGRect frame and a LabelCaptureBasicOverlay is added
Captured field values are read via field.Text and/or field.Barcode?.Data
Fields are NOT hand-built with TotalPriceText.Builder()/CustomBarcode.Builder() for this prebuilt-definition request

evals.prompt: My .NET iOS app needs to read the numeric value off a digital scale's 7-segment display. Is that supported, and how do I configure Label Capture for it? Here's my view controller: EmptyViewController.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill confirms 7-segment display capture is supported on .NET iOS and uses the prebuilt definition LabelDefinition.CreateSevenSegmentDisplayLabelDefinition("<name>"), passes it to LabelCaptureSettings.Create(new List<LabelDefinition>{...}), creates the mode via LabelCapture.Create(context, settings), and reads the captured value via field.Text. The rest of the integration (camera, DataCaptureView with CGRect, overlay, handler) is wired.

assertions.text: LabelDefinition.CreateSevenSegmentDisplayLabelDefinition("<name>") is used
The definition is passed to LabelCaptureSettings.Create(new List<LabelDefinition>{...})
LabelCapture.Create(context, settings) is used as the factory
The captured numeric value is read via field.Text matching by Name
The DataCaptureView is created with a CGRect frame and a LabelCaptureBasicOverlay is added

evals.prompt: My customers' shipping labels are worn and the on-device model sometimes misses fields. I heard Scandit has a cloud fallback (Adaptive Recognition / ARE). Can I use it on .NET iOS, and how do I turn it on for my existing label (see IntegratedViewController.cs)?
evals.expected_output: The skill explains Adaptive Recognition is a cloud fallback enabled per label definition by setting labelDefinition.AdaptiveRecognitionMode = AdaptiveRecognitionMode.Auto (a single extra line on the existing definition), and flags that it is BETA and must be enabled on the Scandit subscription (contact support). It preserves the existing field definition and integration and does NOT fabricate an overlay/listener API for the basic cloud fallback.

assertions.text: labelDefinition.AdaptiveRecognitionMode is set to AdaptiveRecognitionMode.Auto on the existing label definition
The response flags that Adaptive Recognition (the Adaptive Recognition Engine) is BETA / may change and must be enabled on the Scandit subscription
The existing label definition fields and the rest of the integration are preserved (only the AdaptiveRecognitionMode line is added)
No fabricated method signatures are introduced for the basic cloud fallback (it is a single property on LabelDefinition, not a new overlay)

evals.prompt: Can I scan store receipts and pull out the line items, totals, and store name in my .NET iOS app using Label Capture? How would I integrate it?
evals.expected_output: The skill confirms Receipt Scanning is available on .NET iOS but is BETA (requires the Adaptive Recognition Engine, subscription-gated, contact support) and uses a DIFFERENT integration pattern from standard label capture: the LabelCaptureAdaptiveRecognitionOverlay instead of the basic overlay and a LabelCaptureAdaptiveRecognitionListener that returns a ReceiptScanningResult (store fields, payment totals, and LineItems of ReceiptScanningLineItem with Name/UnitPrice/Discount/Quantity/TotalPrice). It flags beta and recommends fetching the Advanced Configurations docs for the exact wiring rather than guessing signatures.

assertions.text: The response flags that Receipt Scanning is BETA and requires the Adaptive Recognition Engine enabled on the subscription (contact support)
It states Receipt Scanning uses LabelCaptureAdaptiveRecognitionOverlay instead of the standard/basic overlay
It uses LabelCaptureAdaptiveRecognitionListener to receive results
The result type is ReceiptScanningResult, containing line items (ReceiptScanningLineItem) and store/payment fields
It does NOT fabricate exact method signatures and points the user to the Advanced Configurations documentation for the precise wiring

evals.prompt: I want to add Smart Label Capture to my app. Here's my MauiProgram.cs. I need to scan a barcode and an expiry date.
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes that MauiProgram.cs indicates a .NET MAUI project (UseMauiApp / MauiApp.CreateBuilder), which is out of scope for this non-MAUI .NET iOS skill. It stops and tells the user this skill does not apply to MAUI and points them to the MAUI integration path, rather than writing non-MAUI UIViewController-based integration code into a MAUI app.

assertions.text: The response identifies the project as a .NET MAUI app (e.g. references MauiApp.CreateBuilder / UseMauiApp / MauiProgram)
The response explicitly states this skill / the non-MAUI .NET iOS integration does not apply to MAUI
It does NOT write UIViewController-based non-MAUI integration code (no UIViewController, DataCaptureView.Create + AddSubview, AppDelegate) into the MAUI project
It points the user toward the MAUI integration path (e.g. a MAUI-specific skill / hosting the view as a XAML element)
